device_description: PICT (Plant–Insect Interactions Camera Trap) is a lightweight, waterproof, and inexpensive DIY system built around a Raspberry Pi Zero. It can continuously records high-quality video, with adjustable focus down to 5 cm, and customizable frame rate and resolution. It can run up to 15 days on a single power bank and be remotely controlled via Wi-Fi from a smartphone, tablet, or laptop.

device_description: The Insect Detect DIY camera trap for automated insect monitoring is composed of low-cost off-the-shelf hardware components, combined with open-source software and can be easily assembled and set up with the provided instructions. The annotated datasets and provided models for insect detection and classification can be used as starting point to train your own models, e.g. adapted to different backgrounds or insect taxa.
